text.title: Platform Engineering Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Backstage portal in production but every 'create service' click ends in a Slack ticket because the orchestration backend was never wired.
antipattern.root-cause: Team built the visible part first because it shipped fast and looked impressive in demos. Backend never funded.
antipattern.fix: Pause portal expansion. Fund the orchestration backend (Crossplane / Humanitec) until at least the first golden path provisions end-to-end without manual ticket.

antipattern.symptom: Backstage + Port + Cortex all running 'to compare'; teams pick one based on whim; three source-of-truth catalogues drift.
antipattern.root-cause: 'We want to evaluate everything before committing' — but evaluation continues past the point where the cost of dual-running exceeds switching cost.
antipattern.fix: Pick one within 2 sprints. Time-box the evaluation. Cancel the other two POCs and migrate users.

antipattern.symptom: Platform team updates the Go template; every service using it sees CI fail on next push; trust collapses.
antipattern.root-cause: Template versioning skipped; updates pushed via 'just sync from main'.
antipattern.fix: Switch to semver tags on templates. Downstream repos pin a version. Updates require an opt-in PR with a generated changelog.

antipattern.symptom: Platform team running on faith; CFO asks for ROI; team produces anecdotes; budget cut next quarter.
antipattern.root-cause: Metrics deferred 'until we have something to measure'; never happens.
antipattern.fix: Ship the metrics dashboard alongside the FIRST golden path. Even a single 'TTFD for new dev' number beats no data.
